reasoning: Based on point-to-point quarterly data up to Q2 2025, Spain has already accumulated approximately 2.1% GDP growth since Q3 2024 (Q4 2024: 0.8%, Q1 2025: 0.6%, Q2 2025: 0.7%), compared to just 0.9% for the Eurozone over the same period (Q4 2024: 0.2%, Q1 2025: 0.6%, Q2 2025: 0.1%). This establishes a massive 1.2 percentage-point gap heading into the final quarter of the resolution period. For the Eurozone to surpass Spain by Q3 2025, Spain would have to experience a severe contraction while the Eurozone simultaneously boomed in a single quarter, which contradicts all available expert forecasts. Forecasters universally project Spain to grow between 2.5% and 3.0% in 2025, significantly outpacing the Eurozone's projected 1.2% growth. Given the mathematical near-certainty derived from the pre-existing Q4 2024–Q2 2025 quarter-on-quarter growth figures, I assign the maximum allowable probability of 0.98.
